columns.name: soak_intensity
columns.description: 0-10 kiln soak intensity setting that lengthens and deepens the high-temperature hold near peak firing.

columns.name: dryer_exit_signature
columns.description: Inline dryer-exit sensor signature summarizing near-infrared and exhaust response as tiles leave pre-drying, used as a noisy proxy for hidden moisture balance in the green bodies.

columns.name: glaze_finish_score
columns.description: Final end-of-line quality score combining glaze smoothness, color development, and defect-free appearance.

stakes.narrative: The tile plant believes stronger soak intensity improves glaze finish by giving the coating more time to level and mature in the kiln. But historical production records were collected while hidden green-body moisture balance was also changing from lot to lot, and when that latent condition was high operators tended to turn soak intensity down to avoid overfiring concerns. The same latent condition also tended to improve final finish on its own, so observational records can make soak intensity look less helpful than it truly is. Controlled firing experiments that set soak intensity directly are therefore critical for estimating its real benefit.

functionals.name: exceedance
functionals.brief_clause: a scrap -- glaze_finish_score below the acceptance line of 24.1 -- is far costlier than a forgone gain

control_surface.deliverable_note: model(regime, n, seed) -> table over [soak_intensity, dryer_exit_signature, glaze_finish_score].
